onboarding.question:
1. Define what "ambient awareness only" means operationally — does the logged-out indicator display text only with no interactive elements, or can it include a subtle visual affordance that hints at sign-in without being clickable?
2. Specify the exact text or messaging string that must appear in the logged-out indicator so the designer knows what label to render.
3. Define what "without visual disruption" means measurably — should the transition from logged-out to authenticated state render in a single pass with no spinner, or is a brief loading state acceptable if it completes within a specific threshold (e.g., under 500ms)?
onboarding.recommendations:
[1]. My recommendation: The logged-out indicator displays text only — no interactive elements, no visual affordances that hint at sign-in, no underlines, icons, or directional cues — it is pure informational text, nothing more.

→ Rationale: Any visual affordance (underline, icon, subtle highlight) signals interactivity or invitation to act, which contradicts "ambient awareness only"; text-only preserves the minimalist principle and ensures the indicator recedes behind the conversation.

→ Note: Pending human PM confirmation before engineering handoff

---

[2]. My recommendation: The logged-out indicator displays exactly "Not signed in" in a single line.

→ Rationale: "Not signed in" is the clearest, most direct statement of authentication state; it is unambiguous and requires no interpretation.

→ Note: Pending human PM confirmation before engineering handoff

---

[3]. My recommendation: "Without visual disruption" means the transition from logged-out to authenticated state must complete with zero loading states, spinners, skeleton screens, or visible re-renders — if authentication state resolution succeeds within 2 seconds, the transition is silent and invisible to the user; conversation history loads into the existing interface without any visual feedback or flicker; the logged-out indicator simply disappears.

→ Rationale: Any loading state, even brief, breaks the perception of seamlessness and feels like a stall; a 2-second threshold allows sufficient time for most auth checks; silent loading respects the minimalist design and keeps focus on the conversation.

→ Note: Pending human PM confirmation before engineering handoff

---

Once you approve these recommendations, I'll update the product spec to reflect each confirmed decision.
